heading: Coupon settlements
label: 1712 Coupon settlements
notes: Effective DateSection applicable to any civil action commenced on or after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2005 Amendment note under .Feb. 18, 2005section 9 of Pub. L. 109–2section 1332 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection applicable to any civil action commenced on or after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2005 Amendment note under .Feb. 18, 2005section 9 of Pub. L. 109–2section 1332 of this title

children.heading: .—Contingent Fees in Coupon Settlements
children.label: a .—Contingent Fees in Coupon Settlements
children.text: If a proposed settlement in a class action provides for a recovery of coupons to a class member, the portion of any attorney’s fee award to class counsel that is attributable to the award of the coupons shall be based on the value to class members of the coupons that are redeemed.

children.heading: Other Attorney’s Fee Awards in Coupon Settlements.—
children.label: b Other Attorney’s Fee Awards in Coupon Settlements.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: If a proposed settlement in a class action provides for a recovery of coupons to class members, and a portion of the recovery of the coupons is not used to determine the attorney’s fee to be paid to class counsel, any attorney’s fee award shall be based upon the amount of time class counsel reasonably expended working on the action.

children.heading: .—Court approval
children.label: 2 .—Court approval
children.text: Any attorney’s fee under this subsection shall be subject to approval by the court and shall include an appropriate attorney’s fee, if any, for obtaining equitable relief, including an injunction, if applicable. Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to prohibit application of a lodestar with a multiplier method of determining attorney’s fees.

children.heading: .—Attorney’s Fee Awards Calculated on a Mixed Basis in Coupon Settlements
children.label: c .—Attorney’s Fee Awards Calculated on a Mixed Basis in Coupon Settlements

children.text: that portion of the attorney’s fee to be paid to class counsel that is based upon a portion of the recovery of the coupons shall be calculated in accordance with subsection (a); and
that portion of the attorney’s fee to be paid to class counsel that is not based upon a portion of the recovery of the coupons shall be calculated in accordance with subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Settlement Valuation Expertise
children.label: d .—Settlement Valuation Expertise
children.text: In a class action involving the awarding of coupons, the court may, in its discretion upon the motion of a party, receive expert testimony from a witness qualified to provide information on the actual value to the class members of the coupons that are redeemed.

children.heading: .—Judicial Scrutiny of Coupon Settlements
children.label: e .—Judicial Scrutiny of Coupon Settlements
children.text: In a proposed settlement under which class members would be awarded coupons, the court may approve the proposed settlement only after a hearing to determine whether, and making a written finding that, the settlement is fair, reasonable, and adequate for class members. The court, in its discretion, may also require that a proposed settlement agreement provide for the distribution of a portion of the value of unclaimed coupons to 1 or more charitable or governmental organizations, as agreed to by the parties. The distribution and redemption of any proceeds under this subsection shall not be used to calculate attorneys’ fees under this section.
